sections.title: What is a delivery note?
sections.body: A delivery note (also called a delivery challan or packing slip) is a document that accompanies a shipment of goods and lists what is being delivered. The recipient checks the goods against it and signs to confirm receipt. It is proof that goods left your premises and reached the customer, and it is the bridge between a sales order and the invoice that follows.

sections.title: Dispatch, confirm, invoice
sections.body: Paper & Pen connects the delivery note to your sales order and invoice so dispatch and billing stay in step.
items: Create a delivery note from a sales order or from scratch
Choose to show prices or quantities only
Send or print it to travel with the goods
Capture proof of delivery when the customer signs
Convert the delivery note to an invoice in one click

sections.title: Delivery note or invoice?
sections.body: A delivery note records what physically moved and confirms receipt, it is not a demand for payment. An invoice requests payment for those goods. Many businesses deliver against a delivery note first, then invoice on agreed terms. Paper & Pen lets you do exactly that, converting a confirmed delivery into an invoice without re-typing anything.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a delivery note and an invoice?
faqs.a: A delivery note lists and confirms goods delivered, with no payment demand. An invoice requests payment. You can convert a delivery note to an invoice in one click.

faqs.q: Is the delivery note maker free?
faqs.a: Yes. Creating, sending and tracking delivery notes is free forever on Paper & Pen.

faqs.q: Can a delivery note hide prices?
faqs.a: Yes. Choose to show prices or quantities only, which is useful when the warehouse or courier should not see pricing.

faqs.q: Does it capture proof of delivery?
faqs.a: Yes. Record who received the goods and when, so a delivery is never disputed later.

faqs.q: Does a delivery note update my stock?
faqs.a: With the optional Inventory module, a delivery note can reduce stock levels automatically as goods leave.
